name: Profile_R5_Communication_R4
title: Cross-version Profile for R5.Communication resource for use in FHIR R4
description: This cross-version profile allows R5 Communication resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Communication resources.

element.short: Cross-version Profile for R5.Communication resource for use in FHIR R4
element.definition: This cross-version profile allows R5 Communication resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Communication resources.

element.short: R5: Who the information is shared with
element.comment:
Element `Communication.recipient` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Communication.recipient` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `Communication.recipient` with unmapped reference targets: Endpoint, Location.
If receipts need to be tracked by an individual, a separate resource instance will need to be created for each recipient.  Multiple recipient communications are intended where either receipts are not tracked (e.g. a mass mail-out) or a receipt is captured in aggregate (all emails confirmed received by a particular time).

Element `Communication.sender` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Communication.sender` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `Communication.sender` with unmapped reference targets: CareTeam, Endpoint.

Element `Communication.payload.content[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Communication.payload.content[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Communication.payload.content[x]` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.
The target context `Communication.payload.content[x]` is a choice-type element and cannot directly hold extensions. The context is moved up to parent element `Communication.payload`.
The content can be codified or textual.  As an example of codified content, when working with machine generated communications, the payload may be drawn from a finite, terminology defined set of communications, especially for short, status update kinds of communications where more of a natural language approach isn't appropriate.

If the content isn't codified, contentCodeableConcept.text can be used.  

When using contentCodeableConcept, the CodeableConcept is what is being communicated and is not a categorization of the content.
